channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Britain’s King Charles visits 9/11 Memorial in New York
item.description: Britain's King Charles III visited the 9/11 Memorial in New York City a day after his address to the US Congress. The visit was a gesture of respect for the victims of the 2001 terrorist attack.

item.title: Trump vows to maintain Iran blockade, Tehran threaten ‘practical’ action
item.description: US President Trump reaffirmed the naval blockade on Iranian ports, calling it more effective than bombing. The move has contributed to soaring oil prices, as tensions between the US and Iran remain high.

item.title: Iran’s rial currency hits record low as shaky ceasefire with US and Israel holds
item.description: Iran's rial currency has hit a record low as a shaky ceasefire with the US and Israel holds. The economic strain on Iran is evident in the currency's decline. Iran’s rial currency hits record low as shaky ceasefire with US and Israel holds AP News.

item.title: Trump Says Iran "Choking Like Stuffed Pig", Refuses To Lift Hormuz Blockade: Report
item.description: US President Trump has refused to lift the blockade on the Strait of Hormuz, calling it his primary source of leverage against Iran. The move has significant implications for global oil markets.

item.title: US Has Spent $25 Billion So Far In War With Iran: Pentagon Official
item.description: The US has spent $25 billion so far in its war with Iran, according to a Pentagon official. The conflict began on February 28 with US strikes against Iranian targets.

item.title: US Israel Iran War News Live Updates: 'Truce must be fully implemented,' says Lebanon as Israeli
item.description: Lebanon called for a truce as Israeli strikes killed 20 in two days. US Israel Iran War News Live Updates: 'Truce must be fully implemented,' says Lebanon as Israeli strikes kill 20 in two days The Times of India.

item.title: Trump, Putin Discuss Iran War And Ukraine In Over 90-Minute Call
item.description: Trump and Putin discussed Iran and Ukraine in a 90-minute call. The presidents "paid particular attention to the situation regarding Iran and in the Persian Gulf". Trump, Putin Discuss Iran War And Ukraine In Over 90-Minute Call.

item.title: US Fed holds rates steady in Powell’s final meeting as Fed chair
item.description: The US Fed kept interest rates steady at 3.5-3.75 percent. Central bank keeps interest rates steady at 3.5-3.75 percent amid inflation concerns and global economic strain. US Fed holds rates steady in Powell’s final meeting as Fed chair.

item.title: US Fed divided at Powell's likely last meeting at helm
item.description: The US Federal Reserve kept interest rates unchanged due to Middle East uncertainty. The US Federal Reserve kept interest rates unchanged due to uncertainty over the Middle East conflict and high energy prices.

item.title: Pentagon chief Hegseth says US ‘watching Iran 24/7’ to track nuclear program
item.description: The Pentagon is monitoring Iran's nuclear program around the clock. Pentagon chief Hegseth says US ‘watching Iran 24/7’ to track nuclear program.

item.title: Ukraine Urges Israel To Seize Vessel Carrying Grains 'Stolen' By Russia
item.description: Ukraine urged Israel to seize a vessel carrying grain allegedly stolen by Russia from eastern Ukrainian regions. Kyiv has repeatedly protested Russian exports of grain from eastern Ukrainian regions. (File).

item.title: Stabbing Of 2 Jewish Men In London Declared Terrorist Attack
item.description: A stabbing of two Jewish men in London was declared a terrorist attack and condemned by politicians. Politicians condemned the attack. Stabbing Of 2 Jewish Men In London Declared Terrorist Attack.

item.title: Trade, Economy, Investment: How UAE's OPEC Exit Will Affect Saudi Relations
item.description: The UAE's OPEC exit may affect its relations with Saudi Arabia, its largest trading partner in the Arab world. Saudi Arabia is the UAE's largest trading partner in the Arab world.

item.title: Oil spikes while stocks slip ahead of US Fed rate decision
item.description: Oil prices jumped ahead of the US Fed rate decision due to fears of a blocked Strait of Hormuz. Oil prices jumped as fears grew that the Strait of Hormuz could stay blocked.

item.title: What that means for the Gulf, energy markets and beyond
item.description: The UAE signaled its intent to pursue independent economic policies and reshape Gulf oil politics. UAE signalling intent to pursue independent economic policies and reshape Gulf oil politics. What that means for the Gulf, energy markets and beyond.

item.title: US-Israel-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: Putin Speaks To Trump, They Discuss Iran And Gulf Situation
item.description: The Iran-US conflict has cost $25 billion so far, according to a Pentagon official, amidst a ceasefire agreement between the US, Israel, and Iran. US Israel Iran War Ceasefire News LIVE Updates:.

item.title: US top court voids Louisiana voting map amid national redistricting fight
item.description: The US Supreme Court voided Louisiana's voting map, a move critics say dilutes minority voting power. Ruling seen as major blow to US Voting Rights Act, with critics saying it could dilute minority voting power.

item.title: Russia’s Victory Day Parade Will Be Missing Tanks and Other Heavy Military Armor
item.description: Russia's Victory Day Parade will not feature tanks or heavy armor due to losses in Ukraine. The absence of tanks and other heavy military vehicles on Red Square will highlight the heavy toll of the war in Ukraine.

item.title: UAE Reviewing Role In Other Groups After OPEC Exit, No Withdrawal Plan Now
item.description: The UAE is reviewing its role in other groups after withdrawing from OPEC on Tuesday. Abu Dhabi announced its withdrawal from OPEC on Tuesday. UAE Reviewing Role In Other Groups After OPEC Exit, No Withdrawal Plan Now.

item.title: Iran has executed at least 21 people since start of war: UN human rights chief
item.description: Iran has executed at least 21 people since the start of the war, according to the UN human rights chief. Iran has executed at least 21 people since start of war: UN human rights chief The Hill.
